turns.user_message: I keep remembering how effortless everything used to feel, like we just naturally fell into step without even trying. Now it feels like I am walking on eggshells every single time we are in the same room. I try to bring up small things, but the air just gets heavy and the conversation dies out completely. It has been dragging on like this for months, and I am just exhausted from constantly guessing what went wrong. I miss the easy laughter we used to share before everything got so complicated.

The silence between us has stretched into something so thick that I barely recognize the person I used to know. I find myself replaying old memories just to remind myself that we actually used to be this close. Every attempt to fix the distance just ends up highlighting how far apart we really are now. I am tired of pretending that everything is fine when the tension is practically vibrating off the walls. It feels like I am holding onto a ghost of what we had while the reality just keeps slipping away.

I look at the space between us and it feels like an entire lifetime has passed since we actually understood each other. The easy rhythm we once shared has completely shattered into this exhausting cycle of walking on eggshells and second-guessing every single word. I keep waiting for a spark of the old warmth to return, but it just feels like I am staring at a locked door. This heavy ache has settled so deep in my chest that I barely remember what it feels like to just breathe easy around them. I am just so incredibly tired of mourning something that is still technically right in front of me.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
